mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Neurodevelopmental disorder with epilepsy and hemochromatosis
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: apathy, increased circulating prolactin concentration, hyperpigmentation of the skin, liver disease, anemia, poor appetite, decreased body weight
renal involvement (not mentioned but often associated), angiokeratomas, neuropathy, cardiac involvement (dyspnea, pleural effusion)
fever, rheumatic symptoms (not explicitly stated), growth failure, poor appetite
facies stigma, impairments of intellectual and adaptive functions, epilepsy (not explicitely stated), retinal hamartomas (may cause papilledema), angiofibromas or other cutaneous lesions
anemia, monoclonal elevation of IgG, bone marrow dysfunction leading to cytopenias
lower limb pain, foot deformities, peripheral nerve involvement causing weakness and numbness
blisters following sun exposure, hypertrichosis, sideroblasts in bone marrow, abdominal symptoms such as colicky abdominal pain, constipation, diarrhoea, nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite
easy bruising, joint hypermobility, vascular fragility potentially leading to hemorrhage, gastrointestinal complications including intussusception, mesenteric ischemia, and rupture
demyelination, psychomotor regression, irritability, seizures, spasticity, optic atrophy, hearing loss
